|Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao City|Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} |Annual festival={{jalink|July|Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{jalink|Shinto shrines|Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{jalink|Yao City|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{jalink|Mount Takayasu|Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{jalink|Yao City|Q490872}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{jalink|Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} and {{jalink|Takami-Musubi| |Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{jalink|Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{jalink|primitive religion|Q1362894|primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{jalink|Kōbō Daishi}}{{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{jalink|divine oracle|Q217123|revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{jalink|Buddhism|Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{jalink|shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{jalink|Meiji period}}, following {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{jalink|Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, and in the 9th year of {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} it became a {{jalink|Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{jalink|Shigi Line|Q874150}} {{jalink|Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069}} * {{jalink|Honji suijaku|Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{jalink|Mount Takayasu|Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{jalink|Yao City|Q490872}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{jalink|Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} and {{jalink|Takami-Musubi| |Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{jalink|Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{jalink|Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}{{jalink|Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{jalink|primitive religion|Q1362894|primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{jalink|Kōbō Daishi}}{{jalink|Kūkai|Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{jalink|divine oracle|Q217123|revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{jalink|Buddhism|Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{jalink|shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{jalink|Meiji period}}, following {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{jalink|Benzaiten|Q818468|Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{jalink|Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, and in the 9th year of {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} it became a {{jalink|Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{jalink|Shigi Line|Q874150}} {{jalink|Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069}} * {{jalink|Honji suijaku|Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill||lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill||lt=Kōbō Daishi|UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill||lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill||lt=Meiji period|UNKNOWN}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill||lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

'大阪府八尾市教興寺字弁天山550', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine}} [[JA:天照大神高座神社・岩戸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442907}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|UNKNOWN}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

|Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

|Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

'大阪府八尾市教興寺字弁天山550', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine}} [[JA:天照大神高座神社・岩戸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442907}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

'大阪府八尾市教興寺字弁天山550', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine}} [[JA:天照大神高座神社・岩戸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442907}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts
From the right side of the stone steps in the back of the photo, one can pass to the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. |Alternate name=Iwaya Benzaiten}} Iwato Shrine is located immediately to the west of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''', and its main hall likewise sits upon bedrock. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}. It is commonly known as “Iwaya Benzaiten” or “Iwatani Benzaiten.” The sacred object is the entire rocky mountain in the area, though previously a carved wooden statue of {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}} served as the sacred object. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Originally, it enshrined {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten}}, an Indian river goddess, and thus had a strong {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|WD=Q748}} influence. From the medieval period onward, the indigenous Japanese water deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the faith of the local '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' underwent {{ill|lt=shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} (syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), and she came to be enshrined as the shrine’s deity. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it attracted the devotion of people in Osaka as a deity of prosperity in business. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}, following {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the {{ill|lt=Benzaiten|Q818468|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten statue}} was moved to the nearby {{ill|lt=Kyōkō-ji Temple|Q11499468|WD=Q11499468}}. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]

|Name=Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine |image=[[Image:Takakura-Jinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (foreground) and honden (upper rear) |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kyōkōji, Benten-yama 550 |Coordinates={{Wikicoord2 dms|34|36|45.5|N|135|38|24.4|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} |Shrine rank=Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded=Relocation: (trad.) Year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} first Sunday |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Amaterasu Ōmikami
Takakura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine''' (あまてらすおおみかみたかくらじんじゃ) and '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) are {{ill|lt=Shinto shrines|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kyōkōji, {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. The two shrines are adjacent, and the boundary within the precincts is not clearly defined; it is possible to pass between their grounds from beside the stone steps of the approach. As a result, to those unfamiliar with the circumstances, it is difficult to notice that they are separate shrines. Because the two shrines are closely related in origin, they are intentionally described together here rather than on separate pages. === Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine === [[image:Takakura-Jinja02.jpg|thumb|right|Torii within the precincts of Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine. The stone pillar beside it bears the inscription “Shikinaisha Takamiza Shrine.”]] Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Mount Takayasu|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140}} in the southeastern part of {{ill|lt=Yao City|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In the Jinmyōchō it is recorded as “{{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao City)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu District}} Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine, two seats, ranked for the Monthly and Niiname Festivals, original name {{ill|lt=Kasuga-gami|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasugato-no-kami}},” from which it is thought that the “Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine” and the “Kasugato Shrine” were once separate shrines. In the past, it was called “Shikinaisha Ōsha Takakura Shrine.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} and {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Ōkami}}. Additionally, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isetsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} are also enshrined. The date of original enshrinement is unknown, but there is a theory that in the 23rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235}} (479), the deity was transferred here from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Ujiyamada Shrine|Q17194122|WD=Q17194122|lt=Ujiyamada}}. Judging from the appearance of the main hall enshrined atop bedrock in a mountain ravine, it is also thought to date back to a much earlier era with origins in {{ill|lt=primitive religion|Q1362894|WD=Q1362894|lt=primitive belief}}. Beside the shrine’s ritual hall is “Shiragohan Falls,” which is said never to run dry even during droughts. {{-}} === Iwato Shrine === {{Shrine |Name=Iwato Shrine |Location=Kyōkōji 550, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=Subsidiary shrine}} (Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine) |Founded= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |image=[[Image:Iwato-Jinja01.jpg|256px]]
Iwato Shrine precincts

It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227}} founded it here after receiving a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|lt=revelation}} from the deity while visiting Takakura Shrine. Until then, there had been no clear distinction between Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine, but in the Taishō period it was separated as a shrine enshrining {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} it became a {{ill|lt=Sessha/Massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|lt=subsidiary shrine within the precincts}} of '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine'''. {{clear}} === Access === * About a 20-minute walk south from {{ill|lt=Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} {{ill|lt=Shigisan-guchi Station|Q7496433|WD=Q7496433}}. === References === * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1436 Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) * [http://www.yaomania.jp/data/InfoDetail.asp?id=1222 Iwato Shrine – Yao City Tourism Database] (Yao City Tourism Association) === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}} * {{ill|lt=Honji suijaku|Q3080781|WD=Q3080781}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Amaterasuomikami Takakurajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:group of structures or buildings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amaterasu Ōmikami Takakura Shrine & Iwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:dyad]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社|大 あまてらすおおみかみたかくらしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages]]
